refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and specifies what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There are no multiple obligations to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit (e.g., ≤ 1%).

candidate_dps: Implement a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a robust data transmission protocol with error correction and retransmission strategies
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters
Incorporate real-time network condition assessment and dynamic adjustment of teleoperation parameters
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
Interaction between adaptive control and teleoperation command processing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring and control functions, supporting modularity and independence. Potential coupling arises from the need for accurate network data and timely control adjustments, but these are manageable within a well-defined interface.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable network condition data within the system
Risk that adaptive control may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of adaptive control mechanisms and specify interfaces for network condition data to ensure modular implementation and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management subsystems responsible for monitoring and controlling communication quality parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send status updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical and logical network layers for monitoring and control signals.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as communication quality monitoring and adaptive control logically belong here; minimal diffusion since this module manages network communication.
Low containment as vehicle focuses on physical operation; would require extensive diffusion to other modules for communication control.
Moderate containment since teleoperation depends on communication quality, but it is more a consumer than controller of communication parameters.
Safety Services may react to communication failures but do not implement monitoring or adaptive control, leading to diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing communication quality monitoring and adaptive control because it directly manages network communication and can contain the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if adaptive control requires teleoperation input or feedback.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data which may come from other modules, requiring well-defined interfaces.
Risk of coupling with Safety Services if communication quality impacts safety functions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication quality monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, aligning well with the requirement for teleoperation support under specified packet loss constraints.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if adaptive control requires teleoperation input or feedback.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data from other modules requiring well-defined interfaces.
Risk of coupling with Safety Services if communication quality impacts safety functions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating good containment of the DP. The module logically encompasses the necessary subsystems for communication quality monitoring and control, minimizing diffusion risk. However, interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services must be carefully managed to avoid diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces, ensuring packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined external interfaces supporting necessary data exchange for monitoring and control.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize interfaces between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and avoid diffusion.
Define protocols for telemetry data acquisition to ensure accuracy and timeliness from external sources.
Establish clear interaction boundaries with Safety Services to handle communication quality impacts on safety without causing coupling.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mappings pass all gates with good containment and verification feasibility, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules necessitate further decomposition. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable more precise definition of interfaces, clearer allocation of responsibilities, and better management of dependencies to mitigate coupling and diffusion risks, ensuring robust safety and performance.
next_level_sub_frs: Monitor communication quality to detect packet loss exceeding thresholds
Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits
Interface with Teleoperation module to exchange control and feedback data
Interface with Safety Services to manage safety impacts of communication quality

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be further decomposed into sub-functions addressing monitoring, adaptation, and interfacing.
Interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services are critical and require explicit functional definition.
open_risks: Potential coupling and diffusion with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Accuracy and timeliness of telemetry data acquisition impacting control effectiveness.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Verification plans for sub-FRs ensuring packet loss control under varying network conditions.
